Title: Phosphate Limitation Responses in Marine Green Algae Are Linked to Reprogramming of the tRNA Epitranscriptome and Codon Usage Bias

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during phosphate limitation; corresponding protein increased in prior proteomics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate limitation associated with increased NPQ and lower Fv/Fm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes photoprotective nonphotochemical quenching and nutrient-stress acclimation under phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; prior proteomics; codon usage bias analysis; comparative functional inference
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRX712380-SRX712411; prior proteomics study referenced from Guo et al. 2018
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Chlamydomonas LHCSR3; strongly enriched in C at the third codon position; central to dissipating excess light energy as heat during reduced growth.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHCSR2 transcripts increase during phosphate limitation. 2. The gene is linked to elevated NPQ and reduced photosystem stress under low P. 3. Codon bias and protein-level support connect LHCSR2 to the proposed tRNA-modification-dependent acclimation mechanism.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher at T12 under P limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports cyclic electron flow and thereby photoprotective acclimation under phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; codon usage bias analysis; phylogenetic reconstruction; comparative functional inference
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified via codon usage bias among P-limitation responders; paper proposes a previously unrecognized role of cyclic electron flow in phosphate-limitation responses.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGR5 was specifically upregulated under phosphate limitation. 2. Its known function in cyclic electron flow links it to efficient NPQ and light-stress acclimation. 3. The study extends P-limitation response mechanisms to include CEF-mediated photoprotection.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during phosphate limitation; protein also increased in prior proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher at T12 under P limitation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate limitation; also previously responsive to UV/high-light stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with light-stress management during phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; prior proteomics; codon usage-associated selection of light-stress genes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRX712380-SRX712411; prior proteomics study referenced from Guo et al. 2018
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ELIP family members are green-lineage-specific and functionally incompletely characterized; FAS-ELIP is one of the responsive proteins highlighted by both transcript and proteome data.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAS-ELIP is induced under phosphate limitation. 2. It belongs to a light-stress protein group linked to the codon-bias acclimation mechanism. 3. Prior proteomics also showed higher abundance under low P.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates cytosine at wobble position 34 of tRNA, exemplified for tRNALeu(CAA), generating m5C-related modification involved in codon recognition
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tRNA containing C34, exemplified by tRNALeu(CAA)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for translation bias of codon-enriched stress-response transcripts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript upregulated under phosphate limitation; protein also increased in prior proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest during P-limited phase (T12) relative to P-replete/P-luxury stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with phosphate-limitation acclimation by supporting selective translation of codon-biased stress-response genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; comparative proteomics reference; phylogenetic ortholog assignment; tRNA repertoire analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRX712380-SRX712411; prior proteomics study referenced from Guo et al. 2018
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as one of two detailed case-study tRNA modification enzymes; homologous yeast function links C34 methylation to preferential decoding of UUG-enriched transcripts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRM4 transcript was strongly upregulated during phosphate limitation in M. commoda. 2. Prior proteomics also detected increased TRM4 protein abundance under P limitation. 3. Its known wobble-position tRNA methylation function supports the proposed mechanism linking tRNA epitranscriptome reprogramming to selective translation during nutrient stress.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of m3C at position 32 in cytosolic tRNAs, inferred from orthology to METTL6 characterized in animals
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: cytosolic tRNAs including tRNAThr/tRNASer/tRNAArg orthologous targets inferred from model systems
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for translation bias of codon-enriched stress-response transcripts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with phosphate-limitation acclimation through putative remodeling of anticodon-loop modifications and preferential codon recognition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic ortholog assignment; comparison to model-organism tRNA modification knowledge
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as second case study; METTL2A ortholog also present but not reported as upregulated. Protein not detected in prior proteomics, likely due to sensitivity limits.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. METTL6 was identified in M. commoda by phylogenetic analysis. 2. METTL6 transcript increased during phosphate limitation. 3. Its known role in m3C32 formation supports the hypothesis that tRNA modification changes contribute to selective translation under nutrient stress.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes phosphate monoesters to release inorganic phosphate for cellular acquisition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for nucleic acids, phospholipids, energy metabolism, and growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among top induced transcripts under phosphate limitation; corresponding protein also highly increased in prior proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly upregulated at T12 under P limitation; reduced after P resupply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate acquisition under P limitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; prior proteomics; phylogenetic reconstruction; codon usage bias analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for external phosphate scavenging under limitation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRX712380-SRX712411; prior proteomics study referenced from Guo et al. 2018
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to the P-repressible alkaline phosphatase PHOX from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii; transcript increased >14,000-fold; phylogeny suggests horizontal gene transfer from bacteria to eukaryotes; codon usage strongly biased at third position.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHOX was one of the most strongly induced genes under phosphate limitation. 2. It encodes an alkaline phosphatase likely releasing inorganic phosphate from organic compounds. 3. Its transcript showed strong third-codon-position bias and corresponding protein accumulation, supporting the link between codon usage bias and nutrient-acclimation protein production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transports phosphate into the cell coupled to sodium ions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for growth and cellular metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very strongly induced under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T12 under P limitation (>2,500-fold)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate uptake under limiting conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic reconstruction; codon usage bias analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for phosphate uptake under limitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the top codon-biased, highly induced phosphate-acquisition genes during P limitation; PNaS family inferred to be ancient eukaryotic with lineage-specific expansions/losses.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PNaS2 was massively induced under phosphate limitation. 2. It encodes a sodium-dependent phosphate symporter linked to phosphate acquisition. 3. Its high induction and codon bias support preferential translation of nutrient-scavenging proteins during P stress.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transports phosphate into the cell coupled to sodium ions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for growth and cellular metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among top induced transcripts under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T12 under P limitation (>6,800-fold)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate uptake under limiting conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic reconstruction; codon usage bias analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for phosphate uptake under limitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the top 10 differentially expressed transcripts in the study; strong third-position C bias connected to proposed tRNA-modification-mediated translation control.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PNaS3 was one of the strongest phosphate-limitation-induced transcripts in M. commoda. 2. It likely mediates sodium-coupled phosphate uptake. 3. Its codon bias and strong induction support the proposed translational acclimation model under P limitation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: likely calcium-mediated activation of downstream light-stress response
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: light stress acclimation linked to nutrient limitation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely promotes LHCSR2 expression based on concordant expression and model-organism evidence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller coordinating NPQ-related acclimation during phosphate limitation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance at T12 during P limitation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate limitation; associated changes in light stress/NPQ state
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Phosphate limitation; likely chloroplast calcium/light signals
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associated with thylakoid signaling network; targeted by STN7/STN8 phosphorylation in model systems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports acclimation to phosphate limitation by linking nutrient stress to photoprotective energy dissipation pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic reconstruction; comparative functional inference from Chlamydomonas
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Appears exclusive to green algae and land plants; codon-biased light-stress-associated responder under P limitation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAS transcripts increased during phosphate limitation. 2. CAS likely regulates LHCSR2-linked photoprotection based on model-organism evidence and coexpression. 3. The study implicates calcium signaling in nutrient-dependent light management.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: state transition-related light acclimation after phosphate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes state-transition machinery and likely CAS phosphorylation after P resupply
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller of post-limitation photosynthetic recovery/light management
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated after P resupply, not during P limitation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher during transition II to P-luxury stages (T29-T39)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Increasing phosphate supply / transition to P-replete conditions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Closely associated with cytochrome b6f complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with light-management recovery processes after phosphate limitation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic reconstruction; comparative functional inference from plants/algae
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not codon-biased; represents a distinct post-resupply response rather than the P-limited codon-bias module.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STN7 transcripts increased after phosphate resupply rather than during limitation. 2. STN7 likely mediates state transitions and CAS phosphorylation in recovery phases. 3. The paper distinguishes this post-resupply light response from the codon-biased P-limitation response.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PSII repair/light acclimation after phosphate resupply
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes PSII repair-related phosphorylation events and likely CAS phosphorylation after P resupply
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller of post-limitation photosynthetic recovery/light management
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated after P resupply, not during P limitation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher during transition II to P-luxury stages (T29-T39)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Increasing phosphate supply / transition to P-replete conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with PSII repair and a distinct light-stress-like response after phosphate limitation is relieved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; phylogenetic reconstruction; comparative functional inference from plants/algae
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not codon-biased; part of the alternative light-management program triggered after phosphate resupply.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STN8 transcripts rose after phosphate resupply. 2. STN8 likely supports PSII repair and CAS-linked signaling during recovery. 3. This supports the study’s conclusion that replete-P transition activates a different light-management program than P limitation.
